mnemonics.title: OOP Pillars
mnemonics.content: Remember OOP principles with: 'CAPIE' - C=Classes, A=Abstraction, P=Polymorphism, I=Inheritance, E=Encapsulation

mnemonics.title: Access Specifiers
mnemonics.content: P-P-P: Private - Parented only; Protected - Passed to children; Public - Publicly accessible to everyone

mnemonics.title: Inheritance Types
mnemonics.content: S-M-H-M: Single, Multiple, Hierarchical, Multilevel - think 'SMHM' as School Management Hierarchy Model

acronyms.usage: Programming paradigm based on objects and classes
Set of methods for communication between software components
Idiomatic technique for hiding implementation details

acronyms.expansion: Single Responsibility, Open-Closed, Liskov Substitution, Interface Segregation, Dependency Inversion
acronyms.usage: Five design principles for writing maintainable OOP code

visualTips.visualization: Think of a capsule (pill) - data and methods wrapped inside, with restricted access like the medicine inside a capsule
Think of a TV remote - you press buttons (interface) without knowing the complex circuitry inside (implementation)
Family tree - children inherit traits (properties) from parents; think Apple tree where apples inherit tree properties
Think of water - can be ice, liquid, or steam (different forms, same substance); a 'draw()' method works differently for different shapes

analogies.analogy: Class is like a blueprint of a house; Object is the actual house built from that blueprint
Like a bank account - you can deposit/withdraw through methods, but can't directly manipulate the balance (data)
Like a child inheriting eye color from parents - gets characteristics from parent class
Like driving a car - you use steering, pedals (abstract interface) without knowing engine mechanics (hidden details)
A person can be a teacher, father, and son simultaneously - same person, different roles (behaviors)
Like setting up a new employee profile - initializing all necessary information when they join
